title: Current U.S. Inflation Rate Is 2.4%: Chart and Why It Matters
author: Sigma Capital

excerpt: The current U.S. inflation rate is 2.4% annually. The Federal Reserve considers this rate acceptable as it aligns with their target of 2%. This rate indicates stable economic conditions.

content.introduction: The current U.S. inflation rate is 2.4% annually. The Federal Reserve considers this rate acceptable as it aligns with their target of 2%. This rate indicates stable economic conditions.
body: The current U.S. inflation rate is 3.3%. The CPI rose 0.3% from January to February. The energy index rose 0.6%, shelter increased 0.2% and
A 2.4% inflation rate is something of no concern at all. The Federal Reserve targets 2% inflation, so we are just about where we want to be.
The consumer price index increased a seasonally adjusted 0.3% for the month, putting the 12-month inflation rate at 2.4%.
keyTakeaways: A 2.4% inflation rate is something of no concern at all
